name: Mike Bowers Homemade Chipotles
recipeInstructions: I prepared these a couple of weeks ago, and it worked well. I used a mixture of peach and hickory; I smoked them for about 8-9 hours then let the fire go out. I finished them in my dehydrator then next morning. Freshly smoked jalapenos are also good (not completely dry-smoked and stored in the freezer). I have a second batch planned for this weekend using red jalapenos from the farmer's market, as suggested in the Chipotle Cookbook. I will freeze a bag of jalapenos and smoke them—this method causes them to collapse and caramelize more, resulting in a sweeter, smokier taste. I use a typical small water smoker, though I've also smoked items in a Weber (small fire on one side, pan of water under the grill). The temperature was likely around 160°F, but if you're interested, I can look it up later. The chiles absorb all the smoke they can in about 4-6 hours. I also smoked serrano peppers and Fresno peppers at the same time.
